Also alignment commands like, taking a selection and aligning them horizontally/vertically or laying them out in a grid.","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Smart Straighten","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"heading","version":1,"tag":"h3"},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"A meta command like ss (canvas.smart-straighten) which looks for pairs of nodes that are connected via edges and have very little delta x,y, straightening those. This is probably nontrivial in the case we have a a lot of connections, but I'm sure there's a way to do it.","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Implicit Grid","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"heading","version":1,"tag":"h3"},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"A canvas mode that snaps nodes into steps (and also a command that gridifies it) so that all nodes are more easily aligned. Maybe with even some nice elastic/ease in anim? maybe maybe not sure","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Smart move","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"heading","version":1,"tag":"h3"},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"If two nodes or more are connected via a straight line, we should be able to hold something like shift or ctrl to move them along the line.","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}
nodes.plain:
Magnetic guides, rules, and snapping
Smart guides and snapping, plus also rulers. Also alignment commands like, taking a selection and aligning them horizontally/vertically or laying them out in a grid.
Smart Straighten
A meta command like ss (canvas.smart-straighten) which looks for pairs of nodes that are connected via edges and have very little delta x,y, straightening those. This is probably nontrivial in the case we have a a lot of connections, but I'm sure there's a way to do it.
Implicit Grid
A canvas mode that snaps nodes into steps (and also a command that gridifies it) so that all nodes are more easily aligned. Maybe with even some nice elastic/ease in anim? maybe maybe not sure
Smart move
If two nodes or more are connected via a straight line, we should be able to hold something like shift or ctrl to move them along the line.

Recognizing circles maybe? and other shapes.

nodes.content: {"root":{"children":[{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Remembering Styles","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"heading","version":1,"tag":"h2"},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Like Miro, when you draw a line for example, or double click one, it remembers the style and applies it to next lines. Same for shapes, etc.","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}
nodes.plain:
Remembering Styles
Like Miro, when you draw a line for example, or double click one, it remembers the style and applies it to next lines. Same for shapes, etc.

Tooltips
Having tooltips on tools may be useful.
